warnings: fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: Fukui reactivity indices for phenol (13 atoms) were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The Fukui indices were derived via finite differences of Mulliken partial charges across the neutral, cationic (+1, mult 2), and anionic (−1, mult 2) species. The most electrophilic site is atom 3 (C) with f⁺ = 2.1028319 (maximum), and the most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (O) with f⁻ = 0.16201477 (maximum). The dual descriptor is largest and positive on ring carbons (atoms 3, 4, 7), consistent with electrophilic character at those positions. The integrity check returned trustworthy: true, with both f⁺ and f⁻ summing exactly to 1.0000. No convergence failures or problematic warnings were reported. The geometry was taken directly from the user-supplied phenol.xyz without re-optimization at the DFT level.
